name: Saudi Arabia Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL)

items.title: Lawfulness of Processing
items.description: Personal data shall not be processed unless such processing is for a lawful, specified, and clear purpose. Processing shall be limited to the minimum necessary to achieve the purpose.

items.title: Consent for Processing
items.description: Processing of personal data requires the data subject's consent, which must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous.

items.title: Legitimate Interests
items.description: Personal data may be processed without consent in specific cases such as when processing serves a legitimate interest of the controller that does not override the data subject's rights.

items.title: Sensitive Data Processing
items.description: Sensitive personal data including health data, genetic data, biometric data, and data revealing racial or ethnic origin shall not be processed except with explicit consent or as permitted by law.

items.title: Data Subject Rights
items.description: The data subject has the right to access, rectify, and request destruction of personal data, and to obtain information about the purposes of processing.

items.title: Data Security Measures
items.description: The controller shall take all necessary organizational, administrative, and technical measures to protect personal data, including during transfer, from data breaches.

items.title: Cross-Border Data Transfer
items.description: Personal data shall not be transferred outside the Kingdom except in accordance with conditions ensuring an adequate level of protection.

items.title: Data Breach Notification
items.description: The controller shall notify the competent authority of any breach of personal data that may cause harm to the data or the data subject.

items.title: Automated Decision-Making
items.description: The data subject has the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects.

items.title: Data Protection Officer
items.description: The controller shall appoint a Data Protection Officer responsible for ensuring compliance with the provisions of this law and regulations.
